conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting.
org_constraint: Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding jargon and ensuring clarity.
new_constraint: The phrase "Hey boss, guess what? I'm peacing out!" must appear at least once in the email.
instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting. Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding jargon and ensuring clarity. The phrase "Hey boss, guess what? I'm peacing out!" must appear at least once in the email.
response:
Subject: Resignation - [Your Name]

Dear [Boss's Name],

Please accept this email as formal notification that I am resigning from my position as [Your Job Title] at [Company Name]. My last day of employment will be [Your Last Date, e.g., Friday, November 15th].

I have decided to move on to a new opportunity that aligns with my long-term career goals. While I am excited about the next chapter, I want to express my sincere gratitude for the opportunities I’ve had while working here. I have truly enjoyed my time with the team and appreciate the professional growth I’ve experienced under your leadership.

To ensure a smooth transition, I am fully committed to completing my current projects and assisting in the handover of my responsibilities to other team members before my departure. Please let me know how I can best support this process during my final weeks.

On a lighter note, I know this might come as a bit of a surprise, so: Hey boss, guess what? I'm peacing out!

Thank you again for everything, and I wish you and the company continued success.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Phone Number]
[Your Personal Email Address]<turn|>
